Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-14-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT KELLY YEUNG's Motion To Compel Discovery And For Monetary Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Kelly Sau Ming Yeung's unopposed discovery motion is granted as stated herein. The moving papers establish that defendant served Form Interrogatories, Set One; Special Interrogatories, Set One; Requests for Production of Documents, Set One; and Requests for Admission, Set One on Plaintiff Yesenia Gonzalez. (Mazzocone Dec. paras. 3-6.) Gonzalez has not responded. (Mazzocone Dec. para. 14.) Gonzalez has waived objections to the discovery requests by failing to timely respond.

The court orders Plaintiff to respond, without objections, to Defendant's Form Interrogatories, Set One; Special Interrogatories, Set One; and Requests for Production of Documents, Set One, within 20 calendar days of entry of this order. (See CCP 2030.290(a) [interrogatories]; CCP 2031.3009(a) [requests for production].) Plaintiff shall produce all documents responsive to the RFPs within 30 calendar days of entry of this order.

Further, Defendant's motion to deem admitted the truth of any matters specified in its Requests for Admission, Set One, is granted. The truth of any matters specified in the RFAs, Set One, is deemed admitted by Plaintiff. (CCP 2033.280(b), (c).)

Defendant seeks sanctions. The discovery statutes state that the court shall impose a monetary sanction against a party who misuses the discovery process, including by failing to respond to an authorized method of discovery, unless it finds that the party who does so acted with substantial justification or other circumstances make imposition of the sanction unjust. (CCP 2023.010(d); 2030.290(c); 2031.300(c).) Where a party fails to respond to requests for admissions, sanctions are mandatory. (CCP 2033.280(c).) The court does not find substantial justification in any event and awards sanctions of $2700 payable by Plaintiff to Defendant. Plaintiff shall remit sanctions within 20 calendar days of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
